3 Highest-Impact Fixes

Not everything - the three changes that move the needle most.

Fix First

Surface the portfolio on the homepage

Your project photos are impressive - they are just buried where most homeowners never find them. Moving 6 photos above the fold converts browsers into callers. This is your single strongest trust signal and it is invisible on the current site.

High Impact

Build individual service pages

One Services dropdown means you rank for nothing. A dedicated page for Kitchen Remodeling in Walnut Creek, Bathroom Remodeling in Danville, and ADU Contractor in San Ramon means you show up when homeowners search those exact terms.

Quick Win

Add schema markup and a clear CTA

LocalBusiness schema takes an afternoon and tells Google exactly who you are and where you work. Pairing it with a phone number and estimate button above the fold stops visitors from bouncing before they ever see your work.

For East Bay Homeowners

What to Know Before Hiring a Remodeling Contractor in the East Bay

Hiring the wrong contractor is one of the most expensive mistakes a homeowner can make. These are the questions that matter - answered straight, without the sales spin.

What should I look for when hiring a general contractor in the East Bay?

The most important factors are licensure, insurance, and a track record of finishing on schedule. In California, every general contractor must hold an active CSLB license - you can verify any contractor's license at cslb.ca.gov before signing anything.

Beyond the license, look for a contractor who gives you a written scope of work, a fixed price, and a realistic timeline they are willing to put in writing. Contractors who work consistently in Contra Costa and Alameda counties will know local permit offices, inspection timelines, and the subcontractors who actually show up. That local knowledge matters more than a low bid from someone unfamiliar with the area.

How much does a kitchen remodel cost in Walnut Creek or Danville?

In the East Bay, a mid-range kitchen remodel typically runs between $60,000 and $120,000 depending on size, materials, and whether the layout changes. A full high-end remodel with custom cabinetry and stone countertops can reach $150,000 or more.

  • Layout changes - moving plumbing or gas lines adds $5,000-$15,000
  • Cabinet quality - stock vs. semi-custom vs. full custom is the biggest single variable
  • Countertop material - quartz vs. marble shifts the number by $3,000-$12,000
  • Permits - Contra Costa County requires permits for most kitchen remodels. Budget $1,500-$3,000 and 3-6 weeks for approval
Be skeptical of bids under $40,000 for a full kitchen remodel in this market. They usually mean unlicensed labor, skipped permits, or a scope that quietly excludes half the work.
How long does a kitchen or bathroom remodel take in the East Bay?

A kitchen remodel with a permit typically takes 8 to 14 weeks from permit approval to final inspection. A bathroom remodel runs 4 to 8 weeks. These timelines assume materials are ordered before demolition starts - a contractor who orders cabinets after demo begins is the reason projects drag on for months.

The biggest time variable in Contra Costa County is the permit process. Walnut Creek and Danville can take 4-8 weeks for approval. A contractor who pulls permits regularly in your city will know how to front-load the process so inspections do not become a bottleneck mid-project.

Do I need a permit for a bathroom remodel or room addition in Contra Costa County?

Room additions always require a permit. There are no exceptions in Contra Costa County.

  • Replacing fixtures in the same location - usually no permit required
  • Moving plumbing, adding electrical circuits, or changing the layout - permit required
  • Converting a half-bath to a full bath - permit required

A licensed general contractor will tell you upfront which permits your project needs and will pull them on your behalf. Unpermitted work can affect your homeowner's insurance and your ability to sell the home.

How do I verify a contractor's license in California?

Go to cslb.ca.gov and search by license number or business name. Confirm three things:

  • The license is active and in good standing - not expired, suspended, or revoked
  • The license classification matches the work - a "B" general contractor license covers most remodeling
  • Workers' compensation insurance is on file - this protects you if a worker is injured on your property
What questions should I ask a contractor before signing anything?
  • Can I see your CSLB license number and proof of insurance? - any legitimate contractor will share these without hesitation
  • Will you pull all required permits? - the answer should always be yes
  • Who are your subcontractors and how long have you worked with them?
  • Can you give me a written schedule with milestone dates?
  • What does your payment schedule look like? - California law limits upfront deposits to 10% or $1,000, whichever is less
  • What happens if you find unexpected issues behind the walls?
Is an ADU worth building in the East Bay?

In most cases, yes. East Bay ADUs rent for $1,800 to $2,800 per month for a one-bedroom unit in cities like Walnut Creek, San Ramon, and Danville. A well-built ADU typically costs $150,000-$250,000 all-in and generates enough income to cover most or all of the construction financing.

California law now requires most cities to approve ADU applications within 60 days. A contractor who has built ADUs in your city will know what the planning department looks for and can design to approval the first time.
